revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kasuga no Iratsume]][[fa:کاسوگا نو اراتسومه]][[ja:春日娘子]][[zh:春日娘子]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Buretsu|qid=Q334644}}|father={{ill|Yūryaku|qid=Q357235}}|relatives={{ill|Prince Hoshikawa no Wakamiya|qid=Q11512901}}; {{ill|Seinei|qid=Q357273}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11088801]] Wikidata name: Kasuga no Iratsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11088801]] Wikidata name: Kasuga no Iratsuko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|春日娘子|date=January 2023}}
{{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |name = Kasuga no Iratsume |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 499–507 |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]] |issue = |royal house = [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] |father = |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = |}}
'''Kasuga no Iratsume''' (? – fl. 507) was [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress of Japan]] as the consort of [[:en:Emperor Buretsu|Emperor Buretsu]].<ref> Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co.</ref>
She is the only Japanese empress consort whose parents are unknown.
==Notes== {{reflist}}
{{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume|Princess Kasuga no Ōiratsume]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Tashiraka|Princess Tashiraka]] | years=499–507}} {{s-end}}

'''Kasuga no Iratsume''' (? – fl. 507) was {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} as the consort of {{ill|Emperor Buretsu|en|Emperor Buretsu|WD=Q334644|lt=Emperor Buretsu|1=Emperor Buretsu}}.<ref> Anston, W.G. (1896). Transactions and Proceedings of The Japan Society, London. Supplement I. Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697. Vol. 1. London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trüber, & Co.</ref>

She is the only Japanese empress consort whose parents are unknown.
